## ThetaDataDx 13.0.0-rc.2

A small follow-up to rc.1 that lands the public-API symmetry cleanup on the Rust surface. Everything here is the last of the cross-binding shape parity work; there are no new endpoints and no behavior changes to existing calls. See the [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/userFRM/ThetaDataDx/blob/v13.0.0-rc.2/CHANGELOG.md) for the full, itemized list.

### Highlights

- `thetadatadx::streaming` is now the canonical module for the streaming surface, and `thetadatadx::market_data` is the canonical module for the standalone market-data client, mirroring it. A Rust example imports the whole streaming or market-data surface from one place.
- `MarketDataClient` is part of the default public Rust surface (previously feature-gated), so the market-data-only client and the type returned by `Client::market_data()` are nameable without a feature flag. This brings Rust to parity with the other bindings.
- The public Rust streaming error type is renamed `FpssError` to `StreamError` (and `FpssErrorKind` to `StreamErrorKind`), matching the `StreamError` name the Python, TypeScript, and C++ bindings already use.
- `Client::flat_files()` returns a `FlatFiles` view that reaches flat files the same way Python, TypeScript, and C++ do, closing the last cross-binding access-shape asymmetry. The standalone free functions remain available as the lower-level API.

### Installing

This is a pre-release, so the package managers will not pick it up unless you ask for it explicitly.

- npm: `npm install thetadatadx@next`
- PyPI: `pip install --pre thetadatadx`
- crates.io: pin `thetadatadx = "=13.0.0-rc.2"`

### Thanks

This cycle was internal API cleanup. Thanks to everyone who tried rc.1 and reported back. If you reported something or sent a patch and you do not see your name, that is on us, not you. Open an issue and we will fix the credit.
